top_candidates.reasoning: The 'beautiful-on-first-hearing speech containing nothing beyond one bounded fruit-set' framing — phrased as 'flowery, syntactically polished, full of bloomed-tree metrics ... yet examined carefully, contains nothing beyond one bounded fruit-set' — directly mirrors yām imāṁ puṣpitāṁ vācaṁ pravadanty avipaścitaḥ ... nānyad astīti vādinaḥ. The 'na anyad asti' closure-clause diagnostic and the avipaścit-as-discrimination-failure framing are both 2.42-specific.
2.43 supplies the predicative content (svarga, bhoga, aiśvarya) but the engineering layer's anchor on 'closure clause' rather than 'fruit content' is more 2.42-specific.
2.46 also scopes Vedic utility (small water-pond before the flood) but its frame is the post-realised state, not the pre-realised partial-reading critique.
